environment: Target values — not tied to a specific machine. Baseline recorded on Apple M-series (darwin), Python 3.14, SQLite WAL mode, local NVMe SSD. CI assertions use lenient bounds that pass on ubuntu-latest runners.
methodology: pytest-benchmark with calibrated rounds. Replay vs live uses perf_counter over 5 iterations. LLM latency baseline uses GPT-4o p50 (800ms) from public API benchmarks. Derived throughput fields (writes_per_sec_derived, reads_per_sec_derived) are computed as int(1000 / p50_write_ms) and int(1_000_000 / read_cursor_mean_us) respectively — they are NOT independently measured; they are arithmetic inverses of the measured latency values.

notes.recording_overhead: Time added to each outbound HTTP call. On a real 800ms GPT-4o call, agent-trace adds 0.09ms = 0.011% overhead.
notes.replay_speedup: Compared against 10 × (800ms LLM + 50ms tool) = 8,500ms simulated live run. Replay completes in 0.57ms measured.
notes.fidelity: 100% of response bytes replayed identically to recorded. Verified byte-for-byte in benchmarks/test_fidelity.py.
notes.fixture_write: SQLite WAL mode with journal_mode=WAL. Supports concurrent test workers reading the same fixture.
notes.derived_throughput: writes_per_sec_derived = int(1000 / fixture_write_p50_ms) = int(1000 / 0.13) = 7692. reads_per_sec_derived = int(1_000_000 / fixture_read_cursor_mean_us) = int(1_000_000 / 21.5) = 46511. These match the README benchmark table figures and are now computed explicitly rather than hand-edited.
